news.summary: Regenxbio (NASDAQ: RGNX) will announce its quarterly earnings on Monday, 2025-05-12, with analysts estimating an EPS of $0.34. The market anticipates news of surpassing estimates and positive guidance for the next quarter, crucial for stock prices. news.summary: Natural Gas Services Gr (NYSE: NGS) is set to announce its quarterly earnings, with analysts predicting an EPS of $0.26. Investors are eager for positive guidance for the next quarter. In the previous quarter, NGS missed EPS by $0.02, leading to a 7.62% share price drop. For Q4 2024, Q3 2024, Q2 2024, and Q1 2024, earnings estimates were $0.25, $0.23, $0.29, and $0.21 per share, respectively. The actual EPS were $0.23, $0.40, $0.34, and $0.41, resulting in share price changes of -8%, 2%, 11%, and 3%, respectively. NGS currently trades at $20.11 per share, down 9.15% over the past year. Analysts stress the importance of company guidance over meeting earnings estimates. This information will aid investors in making informed decisions regarding Natural Gas Services Gr's market performance and potential.
news.headline: Natural Gas Services Gr to announce $0.26 EPS on 2025-05-12. Past quarter miss led to 7.62% share price drop. Shares trading at $20.11, down 9.15% in 52-week period. Analysts watch for guidance impact.

news.summary: HealthEquity, Inc., known as the country's biggest health savings account custodian, is set to reveal its financial performance for the first quarter of fiscal 2026 after the standard stock market closing on Tuesday, June 3, 2025. The company, listed on NASDAQ as HQY, will conduct an investor conference call on the same day at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results. This event provides an opportunity for stakeholders to gain insights into HealthEquity’s progress and achievements during this period.
news.headline: HealthEquity to release first quarter fiscal 2026 results on June 3 after market close. Conference call at 4:30 pm Eastern Time.

news.summary: An upcoming oral presentation will feature comprehensive 12-month clinical data from the INSPIRE Phase 3 trial, along with key findings from patients participating in the open-label extension, including top-line results from 18 and 24 months. This presentation will offer a detailed overview of the trial outcomes, shedding light on the efficacy and safety of the treatment over an extended period. Stay tuned for more in-depth insights and complete results.
news.headline: Applied Therapeutics to present late-breaking abstract at the Peripheral Nerve Society 2025 Annual Meeting including 12-month clinical data from INSPIRE Phase 3 trial and 18-month / 24-month data.

news.summary: In a recent announcement on May 09, 2025, Rogers Sugar Inc. revealed a positive development regarding a deal with Alberta Sugar Beet Growers (ASBG). The corporation, listed as Rogers Sugar (TSX: RSI), disclosed a notable five-year agreement with ASBG for the provision of sugar beets to its Taber sugar refining facility. This newly established supply arrangement is set to span from 2025 to 2029, encompassing the upcoming crops.
news.headline: Rogers Sugar Inc. signs a five-year contract with Alberta Sugar Beet Growers for the supply of sugar beets, covering 2025-2029.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, the Baltimore Children & Youth Fund (BCYF) announced they will be organizing a Learning Lab Community Exhibition on June 3 at Baltimore Unity Hall. The event aims to showcase the ideas and initiatives of youth development leaders in Baltimore. This one-day exhibition will serve as a platform for these leaders to share their innovative projects and promote community engagement. This event is expected to draw a large audience, highlighting the importance of youth development in the city.
news.headline: Baltimore Children & Youth Fund to host one-day Learning Lab Community Exhibition on June 3 at Baltimore Unity Hall to showcase youth work.

news.summary: Labrador Gold Corp. (TSX.V: LAB | OTCQB: NKOSF | FNR: 2N6) based in Toronto reported on May 9, 2025, the resignation of director Kai Hoffmann. Hoffmann has served on the Board of Directors since 2019 and played a significant role during his tenure.
news.headline: LabGold Corp. reports resignation of Director Kai Hoffmann, who served on the board since 2019.

news.summary: A Venture Capital firm based in Canada, ThreeD Capital Inc. (CSE:IDK) (OTCQX:IDKFF), has recently signed a Joint Operating Agreement with its CEO, Sheldon Inwentash, on May 9, 2025. The agreement aims to monetize digital assets held by both parties in the junior resources and disruptive technologies sectors. This move signifies a strategic step for potential financial gains and aligns with the company's focus on seizing investment opportunities.
news.headline: ThreeD Capital Inc. partners with CEO Sheldon Inwentash to monetize digital assets in joint agreement.

news.summary: A recent presentation by the International Guillain-Barré Syndrome Outcomes Study (IGOS) highlighted the real-world evidence (RWE) results indicating improved outcomes with Tanruprubart (formerly ANX005) compared to existing standards of care. The study examined matched patient populations and showed promising results in enhancing patient outcomes in the treatment of Guillain-Barré Syndrome. This presentation marks a significant development in the field, with the potential to positively impact the treatment of this disorder.
news.headline: Annexon presents data on Tanruprubart for GBS at 2025 PNS Meeting: Improved outcomes compared to current care in matched patients.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, Orezone Gold Corporation (TSX: ORE, OTCQX: ORZCF) expressed remorse concerning a fatality at the Stage I hard rock plant construction site of its Bomboré Gold Mine in Vancouver, British Columbia. The incident, transpiring on May 8th, resulted in the death of a contractor's employee due to a vehicle-related accident. Fortunately, no other individuals were harmed in this tragic occurrence.
news.headline: Fatal accident reported at Stage I hard rock plant construction site at Bomboré Gold Mine, Orezone Corp. Contractor employee killed, no others injured.

news.summary: Bitcoin broke above $100,000 on May 8, with $837.80 million in short liquidations in a day, largest since 2021. Ether surged by 22%, triggering $328 million in short liquidations on May 8. Altcoins followed, with XRP nearing $3. Bitcoin faces resistance at $109,588, while Ether eyes $2,850. BNB touched $644 and could head to $680. Solana saw a rise to $180, but may fall below $140. DOGE may rally to $0.25 from $0.21. ADA could hit $1.01 and SUI $5. Chainlink may reach $21.30, and Avalanche $31.73. Profit booking expected at higher levels, risking downward corrections.
news.headline: Crypto price predictions for BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, ADA, SUI, LINK, AVAX. BTC holding $100K crucial. Major liquidations seen. Analysis shows upward trends.

news.summary: This week witnessed significant developments in global markets, with key events including trade advancements, monetary policy updates, and notable achievements in the digital asset sector. On Wall Street, sentiments remained cautiously positive as the Trump administration pushed for favorable trade agreements. A notable summit between U.S. and Chinese officials in Switzerland raised hopes for de-escalation, with investors viewing it as a positive step. Additionally, the U.S. unveiled a trade deal with the U.K., which includes maintaining a 10% baseline tariff, but with plans to reduce duties on U.K. car imports from 25% to 15%. In return, the U.K. reduced tariffs on U.S. vehicles and agricultural duties, bringing levies on American beef and other key exports closer to parity. These developments have stirred optimism in the market, with more details available on financial news platforms.
news.headline: Fed stays put as Trump touts UK trade deal. Bitcoin surpasses $100K amid stagflation fears. Markets optimistic.

news.headline: Over 600 leaders gathered at Purpose Built Communities® conference in Houston for neighborhood transformation.

news.summary: TeraWulf, a mining firm, reported a net loss of $61.4 million in the first quarter of 2025, worse than the $9.6 million loss in the same period last year. Revenue dropped from $42.4 million in Q1 2024 to $34.4 million in Q1 2025, while the cost of revenue rose from $14.4 million to $24.5 million. This surge in costs accounted for 71.4% of total income from operations, compared to 34% in the previous year. TeraWulf attributed the revenue decline to Bitcoin's post-halving economics, increased network difficulty, and severe weather in New York. Other mining firms, like Riot Platforms, also reported losses, with trade tariffs and geopolitical tensions adding uncertainty and cost pressures. Rising network difficulty and trade tariffs on mining hardware are further straining the industry. Miners sold 40% of their BTC in March 2025 due to tariff concerns, the highest monthly liquidation since October 2024.
news.headline: TeraWulf reports $61.4M Q1 loss, $34.4M revenue, blaming Bitcoin halving, network difficulty, and tariffs on mining hardware.
Fed's Hammack: It is reasonable to take a wait-and-see approach.

news.summary: In Buckley, Washington, KB Home (NYSE: KBH) unveiled Enclave at White River, a fresh community consisting of one- and two-story residences. KB Home, a prominent U.S. homebuilder, showcased this latest project which includes modern kitchens with views of spacious great rooms, generous bedroom suites with walk-in closets, and ample storage facilities. Enclave at White River is tailored to suit contemporary living preferences and is expected to attract many prospective homeowners.
news.headline: KB Home opens Enclave at White River in Buckley, Washington w/ 1-2 story homes, modern kitchens, walk-in closets.
